report_metadata.title: 2026 Global Threat Report: Year of the Evasive Adversary
report_metadata.attributed_actor: Multiple actors including SCATTERED SPIDER, PUNK SPIDER, BLOCKADE SPIDER, PRESSURE CHOLLIMA, FANCY BEAR, COZY BEAR, WARP PANDA, OPERATOR PANDA, GRACEFUL SPIDER, VICE SPIDER

iocs.context: A code injection vulnerability in Langflow exploited by Cerber ransomware operators to deploy malware.
An unauthenticated SQL injection vulnerability exploited by OPERATOR PANDA for initial access and RCE.
A file upload vulnerability exploited by PHANTOM PANDA.
An insecure deserialization vulnerability (React2Shell) exploited by VAULT PANDA and GENESIS PANDA.
Part of an exploit chain used by WARP PANDA against VPN appliances for authentication bypass and RCE.
Part of an exploit chain used by WARP PANDA against VPN appliances for authentication bypass and RCE.
A likely exploited vulnerability used by HOLLOW PANDA to compromise Check Point VPN appliances.
A critical command injection vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS exploited by OPERATOR PANDA.
A Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) RCE vulnerability exploited by VEILED PANDA.
A zero-day vulnerability impacting network devices, exploited for initial access.
A zero-day vulnerability impacting network devices, exploited for initial access.
A novel zero-day vulnerability exploited by GRACEFUL SPIDER for data exfiltration.
A zero-day LPE vulnerability in the Windows Common Log File System (CLFS) driver, exploited by VICE SPIDER.
An n-day LPE vulnerability in the Windows Error Reporting service, previously leveraged by VICE SPIDER.
An n-day LPE vulnerability in CLFS, previously leveraged by VICE SPIDER.
An n-day LPE vulnerability in Netlogon (Zerologon), previously leveraged by VICE SPIDER.

mitre_mappings.report_context: CHATTY SPIDER used voice phishing (vishing) campaigns to persuade employees to install RMM tooling.
COZY BEAR used targeted phishing with links to legitimate Microsoft authentication flows to compromise accounts.
A key theme of the report is adversaries using valid credentials, trusted identity flows, and approved SaaS integrations to blend in. 82% of detections were malware-free.

mitre_mappings.technique_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
mitre_mappings.report_context: Threat actors compromised public code repositories like npm to distribute malicious packages (e.g., ShaiHulud).
PRESSURE CHOLLIMA compromised Safe{Wallet} to steal cryptocurrency from Bybit. Another actor compromised the Notepad++ update process.
CHATTY SPIDER persuaded victims to install remote monitoring and management (RMM) tooling for initial access and control.
eCrime and targeted intrusion actors used AiTM phishing kits to gain access to Microsoft 365 accounts by acting as a real-time reverse proxy.
A threat actor leveraged stolen Salesloft OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate data from customers' Drift-integrated SaaS applications.
SCATTERED SPIDER mounted a domain controller's virtual disk to an unmanaged VM to copy the ntds.dit file, effectively performing an offline DCSync.
BLOCKADE SPIDER modified Entra ID conditional access policies and hijacked AD agents to control authentication.
BLOCKADE SPIDER modified a legitimate EDR exclusion rule to apply to all users, creating an unmonitored staging directory.
Post-compromise, IMPERIAL KITTEN conducted basic enumeration of Microsoft 365 accounts, accessing the App Access Panel and Office Home to map services.
HOLLOW PANDA deployed ShadowPad malware via DLL search-order hijacking after gaining initial access to Check Point VPN appliances.
After an initial attempt was blocked, CHATTY SPIDER pivoted to using Google Drive for data exfiltration.
Ransomware adversaries, such as PUNK SPIDER, remotely encrypted files on SMB shares to avoid detection on managed hosts.
PUNK SPIDER remotely encrypts data via Windows Server Message Block (SMB) shares from an unmanaged host.
The report mentions PUNK SPIDER and ODYSSEY SPIDER using AI-generated scripts for post-exploitation. While not specified, PowerShell is a common choice for such activities on Windows.
China-nexus adversaries leveraged various legitimate domain registrars for infrastructure maintenance.
A Russia-nexus threat actor used an AI-generated website to deliver AI-generated and AI-obfuscated malware.
Associated with IOC CVE-2025-3248
Associated with IOC CVE-2025-32706

paths.description: eCrime path focusing on social engineering to gain access, abuse of unmanaged systems, and ransomware deployment.
State-nexus path focusing on exploiting edge devices for long-term intelligence collection.
Cloud-focused path using advanced phishing to steal session tokens for access to SaaS applications.
Supply chain path involving compromise of software packages to gain execution on downstream victims.
